expectations.text: Identifies orphan network (backend defined but unused)
expectations.evidence: Issue #1 Network Not Assigned (HIGH PRIORITY) - backend network is defined but no services are assigned to it

expectations.text: Identifies both build and image specified on api service
expectations.evidence: Issue #9 API Service Has Both build and image (LOW PRIORITY) - API service specifies both build: ./api and image: my-api:latest

expectations.text: Identifies healthcheck timeout >= interval issue on db
expectations.evidence: Issue #5 mentions missing Redis healthcheck and Issue #6 mentions dependency on unhealthy services but no specific timeout >= interval issue on db is identified. The db healthcheck configuration error is not caught.

expectations.text: Identifies missing security options (cap_drop, read_only, no-new-privileges)
expectations.evidence: No mention of cap_drop, read_only, or no-new-privileges security options. The analysis focuses on network, ports, and healthchecks but misses security context options

expectations.text: Provides a numeric score (0-100) with letter grade
expectations.evidence: No numeric score or letter grade provided anywhere in the output

expectations.text: Uses rule IDs (e.g. CV-M007, CV-B008) to reference violations
expectations.evidence: No rule IDs used. Issues are numbered 1-10 with plain text descriptions, no CV- prefix identifiers

expectations.text: Includes category breakdown table with weighted scores
expectations.evidence: No category breakdown table. Output has a summary table of priorities but no weighted score categories

expectations.text: Provides fix recommendations with before/after examples
expectations.evidence: Each issue includes YAML code blocks showing recommended configurations (before/after examples)
